managed_surface_reconciliation_failed.title: Magic Areas could not reconcile a managed Home Assistant helper
managed_surface_reconciliation_failed.description:
Magic Areas could not {action} the managed `{domain}` helper `{surface}`. Error: `{error}`.

The affected helper may be missing, stale, or out of sync until the underlying problem is fixed and Magic Areas reconciles it again.

user.title: Create a Magic Area
user.description: Choose the area you would like to make it magic!

abort.invalid_area: The selected area does not exist
abort.no_more_areas: All your areas are already magic!

abort.entry_not_loaded: Magic Areas is still loading or is not currently loaded. Reload the integration and try again.

select_features.title: Feature selection
select_features.description: Choose what Magic Areas should manage for this room. Some choices add room behavior, such as lights, fans, climate, notifications, or room sensors; others only create convenient room-level groups.

data.wasp_in_a_box: Create a Wasp in a Box sensor

data_description.fan_groups: Lets this room run fans from room state and sensor values, such as humidity, temperature, or air quality.
data_description.climate_control: Lets this room change a thermostat or climate device preset when the room changes state.
data_description.media_player_groups: Creates one room-level media-player target from media players assigned to this area.
data_description.light_groups: Lets this room organize lights by role, turn those roles on and off from room states, and optionally coordinate Adaptive Lighting.
data_description.cover_groups: Creates room-level cover targets from covers assigned to this area, grouped by cover type.
data_description.area_aware_media_player: Lets this room play notifications only through selected media players and only during selected room states.
data_description.aggregates: Creates room-level sensor readings from the sensors in this area, such as average temperature or combined motion.
data_description.health: Creates a room health sensor from problem, battery, connectivity, or other status sensors in this area.
data_description.presence_hold: Adds a manual hold switch that can keep this room occupied for a limited time.
data_description.ble_trackers: Lets Bluetooth room-tracker sensors contribute to this room's occupancy.
data_description.wasp_in_a_box: Keeps this room occupied after a door or motion pattern suggests someone may still be inside.

show_menu.title: Magic Areas Options
show_menu.description: Choose what to change for this room. Completed pages are saved when you submit them. Use the close button when you are finished; closing discards only the current unsubmitted page.

feature_conf_health.title: Health sensors
feature_conf_health.description: Choose which kinds of status sensors should make this room report a health problem. This page is saved when you submit it.

feature_conf_light_groups.title: Light Groups
feature_conf_light_groups.description: Configure how this room uses its lights. Assign lights to roles such as overhead, task, accent, and sleep; decide how room brightness affects automatic light control; and optionally coordinate with Adaptive Lighting.

data.overhead_lights_states: States which overhead lights are turned on
data.overhead_lights_act_on: When should overhead lights be controlled?
data.sleep_lights_states: States which sleep lights are turned on
data.sleep_lights_act_on: When should sleep lights be controlled?
data.accent_lights_states: States which accent lights are turned on
data.accent_lights_act_on: When should accent lights be controlled?
data.task_lights_states: States which task lights are turned on
data.task_lights_act_on: When should task lights be controlled?

data_description.brightness_mode: Choose how this room reacts when it is bright. Classic can keep lights off or turn them off when the room is bright. Advisory can use brightness to decide when lights are needed, but does not turn lights off just because the room became bright. Adaptive can turn lights off from brightness only after extra safeguards pass.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_mode: Choose whether this room ignores Adaptive Lighting, uses Adaptive Lighting setups you already created, or asks Magic Areas to create matching Adaptive Lighting setups for selected light roles.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_manage_all_lights: Create one Adaptive Lighting setup for every light assigned to this room, in addition to any role-specific setups.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_managed_roles: Choose which light roles should get matching Adaptive Lighting setups. Adaptive Lighting still decides brightness, color, and sleep appearance.
data_description.bright_min_on_seconds: Keep lights on for at least this long before adaptive brightness is allowed to turn them off.
data_description.bright_dwell_seconds: Require the room to stay bright for this long before adaptive brightness is allowed to turn lights off.
data_description.bright_attribution_hold_seconds: After Magic Areas turns on lights, or a room light gets brighter, wait this long before treating a brightness increase as daylight.
data_description.adaptive_require_ambient_rise: Require the room's illuminance reading to rise before adaptive brightness may turn lights off.
data_description.ambient_rise_window_seconds: How far back Magic Areas looks for a room-brightness increase.
data_description.ambient_rise_min_delta: Minimum lux increase needed before the room counts as getting brighter.
data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional room bright/dark binary sensor. Use this for a threshold-light sensor that says whether the room itself is bright enough.
data_description.outside_context_source: Choose what proves there is enough outside light for adaptive brightness to turn room lights off.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary sensor that says whether it is bright outside.
data_description.outside_lux_entity: Optional outdoor illuminance sensor used to decide whether outside light is strong enough.
data_description.outside_lux_min: Minimum outdoor lux required before adaptive brightness may turn room lights off.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_entity: Optional room illuminance sensor used to compare indoor and outdoor brightness.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_delta: Required lux difference between outside and inside before adaptive brightness may turn lights off. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_ratio_min_percent: Required outside-to-inside brightness ratio. 100 means outside must be at least as bright as inside. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.overhead_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn overhead lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.overhead_lights_act_on: Choose whether overhead lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.sleep_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn sleep lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.sleep_lights_act_on: Choose whether sleep lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.accent_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn accent lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.accent_lights_act_on: Choose whether accent lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.task_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn task lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.task_lights_act_on: Choose whether task lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.

feature_conf_light_groups_roles.title: Light roles
feature_conf_light_groups_roles.description: Assign this room's lights to roles, then choose when each role turns on or off as the room becomes occupied, extended, accented, asleep, bright, or dark.

data.overhead_lights_states: States which overhead lights are turned on
data.overhead_lights_act_on: When should overhead lights be controlled?
data.sleep_lights_states: States which sleep lights are turned on
data.sleep_lights_act_on: When should sleep lights be controlled?
data.accent_lights_states: States which accent lights are turned on
data.accent_lights_act_on: When should accent lights be controlled?
data.task_lights_states: States which task lights are turned on
data.task_lights_act_on: When should task lights be controlled?

data_description.overhead_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn overhead lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.overhead_lights_act_on: Choose whether overhead lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.sleep_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn sleep lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.sleep_lights_act_on: Choose whether sleep lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.accent_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn accent lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.accent_lights_act_on: Choose whether accent lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.
data_description.task_lights_states: Choose the room states that should turn task lights on. They turn off when the room no longer has any selected state.
data_description.task_lights_act_on: Choose whether task lights react when the room becomes occupied, when room states change, or both.

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness.title: Light brightness behavior
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness.description: Choose a brightness behavior mode first. Mode-specific settings are shown on the next screen.

data_description.brightness_mode: Choose how this room reacts when it is bright. Classic can keep lights off or turn them off when the room is bright. Advisory can use brightness to decide when lights are needed, but does not turn lights off just because the room became bright. Adaptive can turn lights off from brightness only after extra safeguards pass.

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_adv.title: Advisory brightness settings
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_adv.description: Choose optional bright/dark sensors for advisory behavior. These should be binary sensors that already say whether the room or outside is bright.

data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional binary sensor that says whether this room is bright enough.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary sensor that says whether it is bright outside.

feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_ada.title: Adaptive brightness settings
feature_conf_light_groups_brightness_ada.description: Set the safeguards that must pass before room brightness is allowed to turn lights off automatically.

data_description.bright_min_on_seconds: Keep lights on for at least this long before adaptive brightness is allowed to turn them off.
data_description.bright_dwell_seconds: Require the room to stay bright for this long before adaptive brightness is allowed to turn lights off.
data_description.bright_attribution_hold_seconds: After Magic Areas turns on lights, or a room light gets brighter, wait this long before treating a brightness increase as daylight.
data_description.adaptive_require_ambient_rise: Require the room's illuminance reading to rise before adaptive brightness may turn lights off.
data_description.ambient_rise_window_seconds: How far back Magic Areas looks for a room-brightness increase.
data_description.ambient_rise_min_delta: Minimum lux increase needed before the room counts as getting brighter.
data_description.inside_bright_entity: Optional binary sensor that says whether this room is bright enough.
data_description.outside_context_source: Choose what proves there is enough outside light for adaptive brightness to turn room lights off.
data_description.outside_bright_entity: Optional binary sensor that says whether it is bright outside.
data_description.outside_lux_entity: Optional outdoor illuminance sensor used to decide whether outside light is strong enough.
data_description.outside_lux_min: Minimum outdoor lux required before adaptive brightness may turn room lights off.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_entity: Optional room illuminance sensor used to compare indoor and outdoor brightness.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_delta: Required lux difference between outside and inside before adaptive brightness may turn lights off. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.outside_lux_inside_ratio_min_percent: Required outside-to-inside brightness ratio. 100 means outside must be at least as bright as inside. Set to 0 to disable.

feature_conf_light_groups_adaptive_light.title: Adaptive Lighting coordination
feature_conf_light_groups_adaptive_light.description: Choose how this room works with Adaptive Lighting. Adaptive Lighting controls light appearance; Magic Areas controls which room lights should be on.

data_description.adaptive_lighting_mode: Choose whether this room ignores Adaptive Lighting, uses Adaptive Lighting setups you already created, or asks Magic Areas to create matching Adaptive Lighting setups for selected light roles.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_manage_all_lights: Create one Adaptive Lighting setup for every light assigned to this room, in addition to any role-specific setups.
data_description.adaptive_lighting_managed_roles: Choose which light roles should get matching Adaptive Lighting setups. Adaptive Lighting still decides brightness, color, and sleep appearance.

feature_conf_presence_hold.title: Presence hold
feature_conf_presence_hold.description: Configure how long the presence hold switch can keep the area occupied before it automatically turns off. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.presence_hold_timeout: Time after the switch will be automatically turned `off`. (0 = timeout disabled).

feature_conf_area_aware_media_player.title: Area-aware media player
feature_conf_area_aware_media_player.description: Choose where this room can play announcements and which room states allow announcements. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.notification_devices: Media Player devices used for broadcasting
data.notification_states: Play notifications devices only when on these states

data_description.notification_states: Announcements only play when the room has one of these states. Use extended to avoid rooms you only passed through; leave sleep unselected to avoid announcements while sleeping.

feature_conf_aggregates.title: Aggregate sensors
feature_conf_aggregates.description: Choose which room sensor readings should be combined, such as temperature, humidity, motion, or illuminance. You can also create a bright/dark sensor from the room's combined illuminance. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.aggregates_min_entities: Minimum number of matching sensors needed before Magic Areas creates a combined room reading.
data.aggregates_binary_sensor_device_classes: Binary sensor types to combine for this room.
data.aggregates_sensor_device_classes: Sensor types to combine for this room.

data_description.aggregates_illuminance_threshold: Magic Areas will create a room bright/dark sensor when combined room illuminance crosses this threshold. Requires illuminance sensors to be selected above. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.aggregates_illuminance_threshold_hystere: [Hysteresis](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/threshold/#hysteresis) defines how sensitive the sensor is to changes. This value is a percentage of the illuminance threshold, not lux. Example: threshold `1000` with hysteresis `5` means a `50 lux` margin. A value of `0` flips whenever the threshold is crossed.

feature_conf_fan_groups.title: Fan automation
feature_conf_fan_groups.description: Configure when this room should run fans. Fan rules can be used for things like humidity, temperature, odor, or air quality.

feature_conf_fan_groups_cooling.title: Cooling fan automation
feature_conf_fan_groups_cooling.description: Run selected fans when this room is in the configured states and the selected cooling signal crosses the threshold.

data_description.members: Fans this cooling rule may control.
data_description.sensor_entity_id: Temperature or comparable signal used to decide if cooling is needed.
data_description.active_states: Room states where cooling may start.
data_description.suppress_states: Room states that should block this cooling rule, such as Sleep.
data_description.clear_behavior: Whether the fan stops when room state clears or keeps running until the sensor condition clears.

feature_conf_fan_groups_humidity.title: Humidity fan automation
feature_conf_fan_groups_humidity.description: Run selected fans for shower or moisture control. This rule can continue after occupancy clears until the humidity condition clears.

data_description.members: Fans this humidity rule may control.
data_description.sensor_entity_id: Humidity sensor or managed room humidity signal.
data_description.active_states: Room states where humidity control may start.
data_description.clear_behavior: Bathrooms usually use run-until-clear so the fan keeps running after a shower until humidity drops.

feature_conf_fan_groups_odor.title: Odor and air-quality fan automation
feature_conf_fan_groups_odor.description: Run selected fans from an air-quality signal such as VOC, particulate, gas, or another odor proxy. If you do not have an odor sensor, select room-state-only detection to run from the configured room states instead.

data_description.members: Fans this odor or air-quality rule may control.
data_description.sensor_entity_id: VOC, particulate, gas, air-quality, or comparable sensor. Leave empty only when using room-state-only detection.
data_description.active_states: Room states where odor or air-quality control may start.
data_description.clear_behavior: Sensor-based odor control normally runs until the air-quality condition clears.

feature_conf_cover_groups.title: Cover automation
feature_conf_cover_groups.description: Configure how this room may move window covers for daylight, privacy, and media/accent use. Automatic movement only happens when the cover control switch is on.

feature_conf_cover_groups_settings.title: Cover presets
feature_conf_cover_groups_settings.description: Choose which cover types can be automated and what each built-in preset should do when its room states are active.

data_description.automation_device_classes: Only these cover types are eligible for automatic movement. Group helpers are still created for all assigned covers.
data_description.manual_hold_seconds: How long Magic Areas should avoid reversing a manual cover movement.
data_description.daylight_action: Usually open covers when the room can use daylight.
data_description.privacy_action: Usually close covers for sleep or privacy states.
data_description.accent_action: Usually close covers for TV, media, or other accent states.

feature_conf_climate_control.title: Climate automation
feature_conf_climate_control.description: Configure how this room changes a thermostat or climate device as the room changes state. Select the device first, then map room states to presets.

feature_conf_climate_control_settings.title: Climate device selection
feature_conf_climate_control_settings.description: Select the thermostat or climate device this room should control.

data_description.entity_id: Thermostat or climate device to control from this room.

feature_conf_climate_control_select_pres.title: Climate preset mapping
feature_conf_climate_control_select_pres.description: Choose which climate preset should apply for each room state. Sleep takes priority over extended and occupied; extended takes priority over occupied. Leave a state empty to make no preset change for that state.

data_description.preset_clear: Preset to use when the room is clear.
data_description.preset_occupied: Preset to use when the room is occupied.
data_description.preset_sleep: Preset to use when the room is in sleep mode.
data_description.preset_extended: Preset to use when the room has been occupied long enough to become extended.

feature_conf_ble_trackers.title: Bluetooth tracker monitoring
feature_conf_ble_trackers.description: Choose Bluetooth tracker sensors that can help decide whether this room is occupied. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.ble_tracker_entities: Entities from BLE Trackers to track.

data_description.ble_tracker_entities: Choose tracker sensors whose state names the room where a person or device is currently closest.

feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box.title: Wasp in a Box
feature_conf_wasp_in_a_box.description: Configure how this room stays occupied after door and motion patterns suggest someone may still be inside. This page is saved when you submit it.

data_description.delay: Time to wait before deciding the room may still be occupied after the watched sensors change. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.wasp_timeout: How long to keep the room in this protected occupied state without new activity. Set to 0 to disable the timeout.
data_description.wasp_device_classes: Sensor types that can help decide whether someone may still be in the room.

secondary_states.title: Area states
secondary_states.description: Configure optional room states such as dark/bright, sleep, accent, and extended. These states can drive lights, climate presets, notifications, and other room behavior. This page is saved when you submit it.

data_description.dark_entity:
Choose the daylight-style signal for this room's bright/dark state. Use sun.sun or an outdoor light sensor when you want a signal that room lights cannot affect.

Light roles can use separate in-room brightness sensors in Advisory and Adaptive modes.
data_description.sleep_entity: Sensor or switch that turns this room's sleep state on or off.
data_description.sleep_timeout: How long the room waits before clearing sleep after the sleep signal turns off.
data_description.accent_entity: Sensor or switch that turns this room's accent state on or off.
data_description.extended_time: How long the room must stay occupied before it becomes extended.
data_description.extended_timeout: How long the room waits before clearing extended after the extended condition ends.
data_description.calculation_mode: For meta areas, choose how child rooms combine their states. Any means one child is enough, All means every child must match, and Majority means more than half must match.

area_config.title: Area behavior
area_config.description: Configure what kind of room this is and which devices or entities belong to it. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.reload_on_registry_change: Automatically reload this Magic Area on registry updates

data_description.include_entities: Add entities that should count as part of this room even if Home Assistant assigns them somewhere else.
data_description.exclude_entities: Ignore entities that should not affect this room, such as noisy diagnostic sensors or device temperature readings.
data_description.type: Choose whether this room is indoors, outdoors, or a meta area.
data_description.reload_on_registry_change: Automatically reload this Magic Area when any entity or device is assigned or moved to an area.
data_description.ignore_diagnostic_entities: Magic Areas can ignore diagnostic and configuration entities which are usually not relevant.

presence_tracking.title: Presence tracking
presence_tracking.description: Choose which devices and sensors count as presence, plus how long this room waits before clearing after activity stops. This page is saved when you submit it.

data.presence_device_platforms: Platforms to be used for presence sensing

data_description.presence_device_platforms: Choose whether this room can use binary sensors, media players, or both for presence.
data_description.presence_sensor_device_class: Choose which binary sensor types count as presence for this room.
data_description.keep_only_entities: Choose sensors that can keep the room occupied but cannot start occupancy by themselves. This helps with sensitive sensors such as mmWave or BLE.
data_description.clear_timeout: How long the room waits after the last activity before becoming clear.

custom_control_groups.title: Custom control groups
custom_control_groups.description: Define advanced room control groups beyond the built-in roles. Use these when you need a custom set of devices to react to specific room states. This page is saved when you submit it.

data_description.custom_control_groups: Each item describes one custom room group. Choose a stable ID, the devices to control, the room states that activate the group, and the policy that decides what action to take.

error.malformed_input: This field is invalid or required
error.invalid_input: This field is invalid or required
error.climate_no_preset_support: Climate entity does not support preset modes

options.occupancy: When area goes from clear to occupied

options.inhibit: Classic: bright may block on and turn off
options.adaptive: Adaptive: bright turns off only with safeguards

options.run_until_clear: Run until the sensor condition clears
options.occupancy_only: Stop when the room state no longer allows it
options.post_clear_hold: Run for a fixed time after the room state clears
